candidates.title: Decomposing the Geometry of Knowledge Operations: Editing-Attribution Directional Alignment
candidates.summary: Measure TECS (cosine similarity between ROME update and TDA gradient) on GPT-2-XL with 200 CounterFact facts, five null baselines, theoretical rank-one decomposition, and dual-outcome design (positive signal -> dose-response + spectral analysis; null signal -> subspace incommensurability characterization with whitening decomposition). Addresses the Hase et al. localization-editing disconnect at the parameter geometry level.
hypotheses: H1: TECS at l* > Null-A with d > 0.3
H2: Rank-one decomposition correlates with full TECS (rho > 0.7)
H3: TECS predicts editing success after controlling for covariates
H5: Spectral TECS peaks in mid-range singular bands
H6: Whitening explains misalignment if TECS ~ 0
H7: Misalignment is structured, not random
candidates.pilot_focus: Phase 1-3: Fix ROME implementation (EasyEdit), validate TDA gradients, measure TECS against 5 null baselines on 200 CounterFact facts. Decision gate: Cohen's d > 0.2.
candidates.estimated_time: 65-80 min for core measurement, +30-55 min for extensions
candidates.success_probability: 85% for at least one publishable outcome

candidates.title: Multi-Operation Knowledge Fingerprinting via Direction Clustering
candidates.summary: Compute knowledge fingerprints from 5 independent direction probes (ROME, MEMIT, TDA top-1, TDA aggregated, Knowledge Neurons) for 200 facts. Cluster analysis to discover knowledge archetypes. Reframes ambiguous TECS results into a richer typology.
hypotheses: Type-A (all aligned) facts are easier to edit
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute 5 direction vectors for 50 facts at l*, cluster, check silhouette scores

candidates.title: TECS as TDA Confound Detector
candidates.summary: Flip the TECS narrative: high TECS = TDA gradient aligns with ROME's artifact direction = red flag for TDA reliability. Test whether high TECS predicts inflated TDA scores relative to LOO ground truth.
hypotheses: TECS correlates with BM25 surface overlap, not causal influence
High-TECS facts have inflated TDA scores
TECS-as-confound-detector has higher AUROC than TECS-as-validator
candidates.pilot_focus: Correlate TECS with BM25 scores and selective LOO retraining on 10 facts

decision_rationale: Candidate A is the front-runner because it subsumes both positive and negative outcomes through its dual-path design, addresses the most prominent open question in the field (Hase et al. disconnect), and has the highest combined probability of producing a publishable result (~85%). Candidates B and C are held as backups for specific failure modes of A (ambiguous signal and positive-but-non-predictive signal, respectively).
